comparisons.visual: A bare, lamplit chamber. Close walls. A plain wooden chair faces us. NARRATOR sits in it - a lean man of middle years, pale skin, dark hair disordered, waistcoa…
He steadies his hands on his knees. A thin smile.
OLD MAN sits by a shuttered window - elderly, thin, white hair, dressing gown. He turns. One pale blue eye shows a milky film: the vulture eye.
NARRATOR - younger in bearing only by composure, same face and waistcoat - freezes in the doorway under that gaze. His blood drains from his face.
Morning light through a crack in the shutters. NARRATOR enters boldly with a tray, hearty, smiling. OLD MAN in bed, the filmed eye half-lidded.
NARRATOR sets the tray with care. Pats the coverlet. The kindness is perfect - and false.
A dark lantern in NARRATOR's hand, fully closed. Midnight hush. He turns the latch - oh, so gently - and opens the door a hand's breadth.
Pitch black. Shutters close-fastened. NARRATOR eases his head through the opening, then the dark lantern, closed so no light spills. He moves with agonizing slo…
A single thin ray as he undoes the lantern a hair - hinges threaten to creak. The ray seeks the pillow. The OLD MAN's eye is closed in sleep.
He withdraws. The door eases shut.
NARRATOR at the latch again. Sweat at his temple. He chuckles under his breath - a tiny sound - and freezes.
On the bed, OLD MAN stirs suddenly, as if startled. NARRATOR's head is already in. He does not draw back. He pushes the door on steadily, steadily. Black as pit…
Silence. NARRATOR does not move a muscle. Does not breathe.
OLD MAN upright in the dark, listening. Time stretches. A slight groan - low, stifled - mortal terror, not pain.
NARRATOR, head still within the room, waits. Patient. Then - stealthily - opens a very little crevice in the lantern. A single dim ray, like the thread of a spi…
A low, dull, quick sound - such as a watch makes when enveloped in cotton. The beating of the OLD MAN's heart. It quickens. Louder. Louder. NARRATOR holds the l…
He refrains still - some minutes longer - standing frozen while the beat fills the dark.
With a loud yell NARRATOR throws open the lantern and leaps into the room. OLD MAN shrieks once - once only. NARRATOR drags him to the floor and pulls the heavy…
Candlelight. NARRATOR works hastily, in silence. A tub stands ready. He dismembers the corpse - head, arms, legs - methodical, wary. No blood-spot reaches the f…
He smooths the boards. Nothing wrong. Nothing to wash out. A distant BELL sounds four.
Still dark as midnight. A knocking at the street door. NARRATOR descends with a light heart and opens. Three men in police coats and hats: OFFICER REYNOLDS (sen…
NARRATOR smiles - for what has he now to fear?
NARRATOR leads them through with easy hospitality. Lamps raised. Cupboards opened. Nothing amiss.
NARRATOR ushers them in. Shows the old man's treasures - secure, undisturbed. In the enthusiasm of his confidence he brings chairs and places them. He sets his …
The OFFICERS sit. They chat of familiar things. NARRATOR answers cheerily. Singularly at ease. Then - a change. He pales. A hand to his temple.
The ringing becomes more distinct. He talks more freely to shake it off. It gains definiteness - until he knows the noise is not within his ears. A low, dull, q…
He rises. Argues about trifles in a high key, violent gesticulations. The noise steadily increases.
He paces the floor with heavy strides. Foams. Raves. Swears. Swings his chair and grates it upon the boards - but the noise arises over all. Louder - louder - l…
He cannot bear the hypocritical smiles. The beat pounds - again - louder - louder - louder - louder!
He stabs a finger at the floorboards beneath his feet. The OFFICERS rise. The pleasant masks fall. OFFICER REYNOLDS looks down at the planks. OFFICER HAYES alre…
NARRATOR in the chair again. Spent. Staring at us. The lamp gutters. He says nothing more.
